response.overview: Through a series of intimate video interviews, the director personalizes dissimilar experiences belonging to various individuals, assembling them into a layered meta-story. Each testimony—raw, contradictory, and vivid—serves as a mosaic piece. As voices overlap and memories refract, patterns emerge: grief braided with humor, resilience stitched to regret, moments of quiet revelation that bind otherwise unrelated lives. The film blurs lines between subject and storyteller, questioning authorship and the ethics of transformation. What begins as documentary observation becomes an act of imaginative reconstruction, ultimately asking whether truth is a compilation of singular facts or a crafted narrative reflecting the maker's vision.
response.tagline: "Where strangers' truths become a single story."
response.title: Me and my others selves
